title: terminalia oblongata subsp. volucris
intro: Terminalia oblongata subsp. volucris is a plant species commonly known as the Australian or native 'Bully's fig' and is native to Western Australia and the Northern Territory. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine, particularly for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and tannins. The medicinal properties of Terminalia oblongata subsp. volucris are said to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and protecting against oxidative stress. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used in herbal preparations such as infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
